hadiths.ar:
1163  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عُّمَّزُّ ن ْن ُّ إ ُّذ َّي ْن َّه َّ عَّن ْ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال ض َّ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ وَّ ق َّاي َّهُّ 
ن َّعْض   ج َّلْف َّ إ ِمَّامٍ ي َّحْي َّسِث ُّ ن ِالص َّلاَّة ِ ج َّلْق َّهُّ ج َّعَّلَّ مَّا إ َّذْرَّك َّ إ َّو َّلَّ صَّلاَّي ِهِ إ ِن ْ إ َّذْرَّ ك َّ مِن َّ إَّلط  ُّهْزِ إ َّوِ إَّلْعَّصْزِ إ َّوِ إَّلْعِس َّاءِ إَّلْأ ج ِزَّة ِ 
رَّكْعَّي َّي ْن ِ وَّ ق َّاي َّي ْهُّ رَّكْعَّي َّان ِ ق َّزَّإ َّ ق ِى  كُّلِّ رَّكْعَّه ٍ مِم َّا إ َّذْرَّك َّ ج َّلْف َّ إَّلْأ ِمَّامِ ق ِى  ن َّق ْسِهِ ن ِا ُّمِّ إَّلْكِ ي َّاب ِ ، ق َّا ِذ َّإ سَّل َّمَّ إَّلْأ ِمَّامُّ ق َّامَّ 
ق َّصَّل َّى إَّلْأ َّج ِ ي زَّي َّي ْن ِ لأَّ ي َّق ْزَّإ ُّ ق ِي هِمَّا إ ِن  َّمَّا هُّوَّ ي َّسْن ِي ح  وَّ ي َّهْلِي ل  وَّ ذُّعَّاء  لَّي ْسَّ ق ِي هِمَّا ق ِزَّإءَّة   وَّ إ ِن ْ إ َّذْرَّك َّ رَّ كْعَّه ً ق َّزَّإ َّ ق ِي هَّا ج َّلْف َّ  
إَّلْأ ِمَّامِ ق َّا ِذ َّإ سَّل َّمَّ إَّلْأ ِمَّامُّ ق َّامَّ ق َّق َّزَّإ َّ إ ُّم َّ إَّلْكِي َّاب ِ، ي ُّم َّ ق َّعَّذَّ ق َّي َّس َّه َّذَّ ي ُّم َّ  ق َّامَّ ق َّصَّل َّى رَّكْعَّي َّي ْن ِ لَّي ْسَّ ق ِي هِمَّا ق ِزَّإءَّة   .
hadiths.en:
Bakr ibn Muhammad Al-Azdi narrated by Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq 
{a.s} that He said: "I dislike it for a person to pray behind the Imam in a prayer where the 
recitation is not audible, and he stands there like a donkey."  
I (Bakr) asked: "May I be your ransom, what should he do then?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "He should engage in tasbih (glorification)."  
[REFERENCES] Qurb al-Isnād, Part 1, p. 37 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 392 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, 
Part 3, p. 276 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1205 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 360 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 79 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1162
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٦٢

hadiths.ar:
1164   -  وَّ رَّوَّى عُّن َّي ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ُّ عَّلِى ٍّ إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى  ُّ عَّن ْ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ: س ا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّكُّون ُّ 
ج َّلْف َّ إَّلْأ ِمَّامِ ق َّي ُّطِي لُّ إَّلْأ ِمَّامُّ إَّلي  َّس َّه ُّذَّ ق َّال ن ْ إ َّجَّث  َّ .
hadiths.en:
Umar ibn Udhayna narrated from Zurarah by Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -
Baqir {a.s} that He said:  
"If a person joins a congregational prayer after some of it has passed but counts their 
prayer as valid behind the Imam, then what they have caught counts as the beginning 
of their prayer.  
For example, if they join the Dhuhr, Asr, or Isha prayers and catch two rak'ahs and miss 
two, they should recite Surah Al -Fatiha (the Mother of the Book) quietly in each of the 
two rak'ahs they perform behind the Imam.  
When the Imam completes the prayer (with taslim), the individual should stand and 
complete their remaining two rak'ahs, during which they do not recite but engage in 
tasbih (glorification), tahleel (saying 'La ilaha illAllah {SWT}'), and supplication without 
recitation. If the person joins and catches one rak'ah, they should recite Surah Al -
Fatiha quietly in that rak'ah behind the Imam.  
When the Imam finishes with taslim, the individual should stand, recite Surah Al -
Fatiha, sit for tashahhud, then stand and complete two more rak'ahs without 
recitation." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1163
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٦٣

hadiths.ar:
1165  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ إ ِسْحَّاق ُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّم َّارٍ ق َّالَّ لَّهُّ: إ َّذْج ُّلُّ إَّلْمَّسْح ِذَّ وَّ ق َّذْ رَّكَّغَّ إَّلْأ ِمَّامُّ ق َّا َّرْكَّغُّ ن ِزُّكُّوعِهِ وَّ إ ن َّا وَّجْذِى  وَّ إ َّسْح ُّذُّ  
ق َّا ِذ َّإ رَّق َّعْث ُّ رَّإ ْسِى  ق َّا َّى  َّ س َّى ْءٍ إ َّصْن َّغُّ ق َّال ق ُّمْ مَّعَّهُّمْ وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان ُّوإ ج ُّلُّوساً ق َّاج ْلِسْ 
مَّعَّهُّمْ .
hadiths.en:
Ubaydullah ibn Ali Al -Halabi narrated from Zurarah from Imam Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
who said: I asked him about a person who is behind the Imam, and the Imam prolongs 
the tashahhud (sitting for testimony).  
Imam {a.s} said: "He may perform the taslim (salutation of peace) and leave if he 
wishes."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 393 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 317 • Tahdhib Al -
Ahkam, Part 2, p. 349 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1273 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 416 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 413 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1164
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٦٤

hadiths.ar:
1166   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ سَّمَّاعَّه :  عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّا ْي ِى  إَّلْمَّسْح ِذَّ وَّ ق َّذْ صَّل َّى إ َّهْلُّهُّ ي َّن ْذَّإ ُّ ن ِالْمَّكْي ُّون َّه ِ إ َّوْ ي َّي َّطَّو َّعُّ ق َّق َّال ى  
وَّق ْث ٍ جَّسَّن ٍ ق َّلاَّ ن َّا ْسَّ ن ِالي  َّطَّو ُّعِ ق َّن ْلَّ إَّلْق َّزِي ص َّه ِ وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ ج َّاف َّ ج ُّزُّوح َّ إَّلْوَّق ْث ِ إ َّج  َّزَّةُّ وَّ لْي َّن ْذَّإ ْ   ن ِ الْق َّزِي ص َّه ِ وَّ هُّوَّ جَّق  ُّ إَّلل َّهِ 
عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ي ُّم َّ لْي َّي َّطَّو َّعْ مَّا س َّاءَّ .
hadiths.en:
Ishaq ibn Ammar asked Imam {a.s}: "I enter the mosque, and the Imam is 
already in the bowing position (ruku'), so I bow with him while I am alone and then 
prostrate. When I lift my head, what should I do next?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "Stand up and go to join them (the congregation). If they are standing, 
then stand with them, and if they are sitting, then sit with them."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 394 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 3, p. 281 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 
8, p. 1196 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 386 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1165
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٦٥

hadiths.ar:
1167   -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّجَّذِهِمَّا عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّم :  ق ِى  إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّذْج ُّلُّ إَّلْمَّسْح ِذَّ ق َّي َّح َّاف ُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّق ُّوي َّهُّ إَّلز َّكْعَّه ُّ 
ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Sama’a asked the Imam {a.s} about a man who enters the mosque and finds 
that the congregation has already prayed. Should he begin with the obligatory (fardh) 
prayer or offer a voluntary (nafl) prayer?  
Imam {a.s} said: "If there is still ample time, there is no harm in offering a voluntary 
prayer before the obligatory one. However, if he fears that the time for the obligatory 
prayer will end, he should prioritize the obligatory prayer, as it is the right of Allah {SWT}, 
the Almighty. Afterward, he may offer as many voluntary prayers as he wishes."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1166
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٦٦

hadiths.ar:
1168  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مُّ ن ْن ُّ مَّي ْمُّون ٍ عَّن ِ إَّلص َّاذِق ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  ق ِى  إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّو ُّم ُّ إَّلن ِّسَّاءَّ لَّي ْسَّ مَّعَّهُّن  َّ رَّج ُّل  ق ِى  إَّلْق َّزِي ص َّه ِ 
ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from one of the Imams {a.s} regarding a man 
who enters the mosque and fears that he will miss the rak'ah (unit of prayer).  
Imam {a.s} said: "He should bow (in ruku') before reaching the congregation and then 
walk while in the bowing position until he joins them."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 394 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 44 • Al-Istibsar, Part 
1, p. 436 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1196 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 384 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1167
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٦٧

hadiths.en:
Ibrahim ibn Maymun narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
regarding a man leading women in the obligatory prayer, with no other man present.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, he may lead them, and if there is a boy present, he should stand 
beside him." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 377 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 394 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Part 3, p. 268 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1221 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 333 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 342 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1168
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٦٨

hadiths.ar:
1170 - وَّ كَّان َّ إ َّمِي ز إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّق ُّول ي  َّى ي َّحْي َّلِمَّ 
ق َّا ِن ْ إ َّم َّ ج َّار َّب ْ صَّلاَّي ُّهُّ وَّ ق َّسَّذَّب ْ صَّلاَّة ُّ مَّن ْ ي ُّصَّلِّى  ج َّلْق َّهُّ .
hadiths.en:
Ammar Al-Sabati narrated that he asked Imam {a.s} about a man who gives the 
adhan (call to prayer) and iqamah (call to commence the prayer) intending to pray 
alone. Then another person arrives and asks to pray in congregation. He asked 
whether they may use the same adhan and iqamah.  
Imam {a.s} replied: "No, rather he should give the adhan and iqamah again."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 394 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 3, p. 282 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 
7, p. 605 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 432 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1169
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٦٩

hadiths.ar:
1171  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّم َّار  إَّلس َّان َّاطِى  ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ إ َّذْرَّك َّ إَّلْأ ِمَّامَّ جِي ن َّ ي ُّسَّلِّمُّ ق َّال 
وَّ ي ُّق ِي مَّ وَّ ي َّق ْي َّي ِحَّ إَّلص َّلاَّة َّ .
hadiths.en:
The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said:  
"There is no problem if a young boy (who has not yet reached puberty) gives the adhan 
(call to prayer), but he should not lead the prayer until he has reached puberty. If he 
leads, his own prayer is valid, but the prayer of those who follow him is invalid. "  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 395 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 29 • Al-Istibsar, Part 
1, p. 423 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1179 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 322 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1170
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٧٠

hadiths.ar:
1172  -  وَّ سُّن ِل : عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّا ْي ِى  إَّلْمَّسْح ِذَّ وَّ هُّمْ ق ِى  إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ وَّ ق َّذْ سَّن َّق َّهُّ إَّلْأ ِمَّامُّ ن ِزَّكْعَّه ٍ ق َّي ُّكَّن ِّزُّ ق َّي َّعْي َّل ُّ  إَّلْأ ِمَّامُّ ق َّي َّا ْج ُّذ ُّ  
ن ِي َّذِةِ وَّ ي َّكُّون ُّ إ َّذْن َّى إَّلْق َّوْمِ إ ِلَّي ْهِ ق َّي ُّق َّذِّمُّهُّ ق َّق َّالَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم إَّلص َّلاَّة َّ ي ُّم َّ ي َّح ْلِسُّ جَّي  َّى إ ِذ َّإ ق َّزَّع ُّوإ مِن َّ إَّلي  َّس َّه ُّذِ  
إ َّوْمَّا َّ ن ِي َّذِةِ عَّن ِ إَّلْي َّمِي ن ِ وَّ إَّلس ِّمَّالِ وَّ كَّان َّ ذ َّلِك َّ إَّل َّذ ِى  ي ُّومِى ُّ ن ِي َّذِةِ إَّلي  َّسْلِي مَّ إ َّوْ ي ُّق ْص َّى صَّلاَّي ُّ هُّمْ وَّ إ َّي َّم َّ هُّوَّ مَّا كَّان َّ ق َّاي َّهُّ .
hadiths.en:
Ammar al-Sabati asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} about a man 
who arrives at the mosque just as the Imam is concluding the prayer with the taslim 
(final salutation).  
Imam {a.s} replied: "He should give the adhan (call to prayer), make the iqamah (call to 
commence the prayer), and then begin his prayer."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 395 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 609 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1235 • 
Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 431 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1171
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٧١

hadiths.en:
A question was asked about a man who enters the mosque while the 
congregation is already in prayer, and the Imam is ahead of him by one rak'ah. He 
pronounces the takbir, and then the Imam becomes unable to continue (due to illness 
or another reason) and takes the man’s hand (indicating him to lead), and he happens 
to be the nearest person to the Imam.  
Imam {a.s} said: "He should lead them in completing the prayer, then sit until they finish 
their tashahhud (final testimony). He should gesture to the right and left with his hand 
as a sign of completing the prayer (taslim) or wait until the prayer is completed by t he 
congregation, after which he can complete what he missed of his own prayer."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1172
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٧٢

hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Sahl narrated from his father who said:  
“I asked Imam Al -Ridha {a.s} about a person who bows (in ruku') with an Imam of a 
congregation who is followed, and then raises his head before the Imam.”  
Imam {a.s} replied: "He should repeat his bowing (ruku') with him."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1173
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٧٣

hadiths.ar:
1175   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ُّ ن ْن ُّ ي َّسَّارٍ إ َّن  َّهُّ سَّمِغَّ مَّن ْ ي َّسْا َّلُّ إَّلزِّص َّا عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ صَّل َّى إ ِلَّى ج َّان ِث ِ رَّج ُّلٍ ق َّق َّامَّ 
عَّن ْ ي َّسَّارِةِ وَّ هُّوَّ لأَّ ي َّعْلَّمُّ كَّي ْف َّ ي َّصْن َّغُّ إ ِذ َّإ عَّلِمَّ وَّ هُّوَّ ق ِى  إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Al-Fudayl ibn Yasar asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about a 
man who prays behind an Imam whom he is following and raises his head from 
prostration (Sujood) before the Imam raises his head.  
Imam {a.s} said: "He should prostrate again." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 396 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1255 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1174
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٧٤

hadiths.ar:
1176  -  وَّق َّال إ َّمِي زُّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
ي َّزْق َّعْن َّ رُّءُّوسَّهُّن  َّ ق َّن ْلَّ إَّلزِّج َّالِ لِص ِي ق ِ إَّلْأ ُّر ُّرِ .
hadiths.en:
Al-Hussain ibn Yasar narrated that he heard someone asking Imam Al -Ridha 
{a.s} about a man who was praying next to another man and stood on his left side without 
knowing what to do upon realizing this during the prayer.  
Imam {a.s} said: "He should move him to his right side." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1175
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٧٥

hadiths.en:
The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said:  
"The women used to pray with The Prophet {saws}, and they were instructed not to raise 
their heads (from prostration) before the men due to the tightness of the garments”.  
[REFERENCES] Qurb al-Isnad, Part 1, p. 18 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 396 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 
1223 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 343 • Wasail Al-Shia, V. 8, p. 343 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 42 • Mustadrak 
Al-Wasail, Vol. 6, p. 511 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1176
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٧٦

hadiths.en:
Hisham ibn Salim asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about 
whether a woman can lead other women in prayer.  
Imam {a.s} said: "She may lead them in the nafl (supererogatory) prayers, but not in the 
obligatory (fardh) prayers. Moreover, She should not stand in front of them; rather, She 
should stand in their midst."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 396 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 205 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1177
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٧٧

hadiths.ar:
1179 - وَّ رَّوَّى هِس َّامُّ ن ْن ُّ سَّالِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
ن َّي ْي ِهَّا وَّ صَّلاَّي ُّهَّا ق ِى  ن َّي ْي ِهَّا إ َّق ْص َّلُّ مِن ْ صَّلاَّي ِهَّا ق ِى  إَّلذ َّإرِ .  
وإلزج ل إ ذ إ إ م إلمزإ ة  كان ث  ج لق ه عن  ي مي ن ه سح وذها مغ ركن ي ي ه .
hadiths.en:
Zurarah narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} that He said:  
“I asked him whether a woman can lead other women in prayer.”  
Imam {a.s} said: "No, except for the prayer over the deceased (funeral prayer) if there is 
no one more appropriate than her. In that case, She should stand among them in the 
row, and She recites the takbir and they follow her."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, V. 1, p. 397 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, P 3, p. 206 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, 
P 3, p. 268 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 326 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 331 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 427 
• Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1225 • Al-Wafi, V. 24, p. 418 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 117 • Wasail Al-Shia, V. 8, p. 334 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1178
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٧٨

hadiths.ar:
1180  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى :  عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّو ُّم ُّ إَّلن ِّسَّاءَّ ق َّال  
كَّان ُّوإ عَّن ِي ذإً .
hadiths.en:
Hisham ibn Salim narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s}:  
"A woman's prayer in her private chamber (mikhdah) is better than her prayer in her 
house, and her prayer in her house is better than her prayer in the courtyard."  
[AL SADUQ] 
Also, when a man leads a woman in prayer, She should stand behind him on his right, 
with her prostration aligning with his knees.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 397 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 515 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1179
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٧٩

hadiths.en:
Al-Halabi asked Imam {a.s} about a man leading women in prayer.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, he may lead them, and if there are young boys among them, 
position them in front of the women, even if they are servants."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 397 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 343 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1180
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٨٠

hadiths.en:
Dawud ibn Al-Hussain narrated that the Imam {a.s} said:  
"A resident (non -traveler) should not lead a traveler, nor should a traveler lead a 
resident. If a man finds himself in a situation where he must lead a congregation of 
residents and completes two rak'ahs, he should then offer the taslim (salutation of 
peace), take the hand of one of the residents, and place him to lead the rest. When a 
traveler prays behind a group of residents, he should complete his prayer with two 
rak'ahs and then offer the taslim." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1181
